回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	113學年度第2學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 西洋戲劇 (Western Drama)	開課班級 : 應英三年級	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 陳宛利	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
– to gain knowledge about a variety of dramatic elements, theatrical techniques and conventions --to enhance speaking skills and motivation via performing drama -to experience and value drama as a social, collaborative and creative art form - to develop comprehension abilities, analytical and synthesizing skills via drama - to promote mutual collaboration through peer and group activities 


2.教學綱要
This course is designed to develop students’ reading and speaking skills through reading landmarks of drama and conducting collaborative drama activities. We cannot launch this introductory course to Western drama without tackling William Shakespeare, a must-read for all English majors. Among the Shakespearean plays, _Romeo and Juliet_ presents challenging responses to the feudal-aristocratic society of medieval England and dovetails with the spirit of the Renaissance (14th~17th) that turns the eyes inward towards human nature and what constructs social order. Shakespeare’s works receive quite amount of movie adaptations, and we will read _Romeo and Juliet_ with _Shakespeare in Love_, a 1998 film based on the play.

The second half of the semester will be devoted to _Waiting for Godot_, the award-winning work of Samuel Beckett, recipient of the 1969 Nobel Prize in Literature. His minimal theater lays bare of dramatic elements and theatrical techniques, and provides us with insights into the philosophical movement in the 20th century known as existentialism. Course reading materials along with the designed activities of drama performance will establish springboards for critical readings of literature, develop analytical reading skills, and produce critical responses. Teaching literature to adult language learners both enhance the effectiveness of language learning, and develop literary appreciation and literacy skills.

Selected quotes:

-"What's in a name? A rose
By any other name would smell as sweet.
So Romeo would still be perfect,
If he were not called Montague.
Romeo, take off your name.
And for that name, which is no part of you,
Take all of me." (Shakespeare, _Romeo and Juliet_)

-“Estragon: We always find something, eh Didi, to give us the impression we exist?" (Beckett, _Waiting for Godot_)

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Assessment: Quiz, attendance & in-class performance, Midterm Exam (30%); Final Project: group drama performances (30%) 
Course Requirements: Students are expected to attend all the class sessions and attend each class with course materials. Late arrival, coming to class empty-handed, or being a phubber in class will lead to a deduction of the final grade. Be on time and come prepared to learn and contribute. Students will be selected at random to answer questions and be graded based on how well they demonstrate understandings of the reading. No make-up quizzes for your absences. Understand and be responsible for your grade. To facilitate learning outside the classroom, course materials and assignments will be uploaded onto E-campus, an E-learning environment for all NQU courses. All students must resister for E-campus and also enter a frequently used email account in order to gain full access to course materials, to receive course updates, to form small groups, and to submit homework online. In an early prevention of an unexpected internet breakdown, please be advised to download weekly reading materials in advance. For course-related questions, contact me at tmpmlily@nqu.edu.tw
